label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is NOT a reason the English language spread to numerous parts of the world. The passage starts by stating that English was introduced to the Americas by British colonization, beginning in 1607. Then it says the language spread to other parts because of British trade and colonization, and the spread of the former British Empire, which had a large population by 1921. So the reasons given are British colonization, British trade, and the spread of the British Empire. The options are (A) British colonization, (B) the spread of the former English Empire (which is the same as the British Empire), (C) British trade, and (D) a large British population.
